subject: [PATCH v2 1/1] writeback: don't block sync(2) for filesystems with no data integrity guarantees

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary:
The reviewer, David (Arm), agrees with the patch and finds it sensible.

Reviewer David suggested moving the handling of SB_I_NO_DATA_INTEGRITY superblock flag to sync_inodes_sb() or adding a warning to it, to catch existing and future callers.
reviews.raw_body:
Makes sense to me.

[...]

---

I was wondering whether that handling should be moved to
sync_inodes_sb(), so it would catch any (existing+future) callers.

Alternatively, we could catch abuse by adding a warning to sync_inodes_sb.

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author addressed a concern about the potential for deadlock on fuse filesystems when sync(2) is called, explaining that the patch's behavior is consistent with the original implementation before tmp folios were removed and that the issue was already present in the previous code. The author does not indicate any plans to revise the patch.

Author agrees to restructure the code by bringing back a previously removed comment block, which will help clarify the logic for filesystems without data integrity guarantees.

Author acknowledged that the patch's approach to skipping writeback completion in sync(2) may not be consistent across all callers, and agreed to restructure the code in v3 to address this issue.
reviews.raw_body:
On Thu, Mar 19, 2026 at 2:26 PM David Hildenbrand (Arm)
<david@kernel.org> wrote:

---

That's a good idea - I'll bring back the comment block that was
previously in wait_sb_inodes() and move it here

---

Thinking about this some more, I think you're right.

At the vfs layer, the only other relevant callers are
generic_shutdown_super() when handling an unmount and syncfs for
syncing a specific fd. For these, I was thinking it'd be more useful
to return after writeback has actually completed, since the caller has
to directly invoke the operation on a specific fd/mount. But that's
wrong, we shouldn't be picking and choosing which syncs are ok vs not
ok to wait for. And I'm realizing now there's also the
hibernate/suspend call paths that call filesystems_freeze_callback()
which can also call into sync_filesystem() -> sync_inodes_sb()...

I'll make this change for v3.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-03-19.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new superblock flag, SB_I_NO_DATA_INTEGRITY, to indicate filesystems that cannot guarantee data persistence on sync. For such filesystems, the sync(2) system call will initiate writeback of dirty inodes but will not wait for the flusher threads to complete the writeback. This change is necessary to prevent hangs seen on fuse-overlayfs when the user session cgroups are frozen before invoking kernel suspend. The patch replaces a per-inode flag with a superblock-level flag, allowing for more efficient handling of data integrity guarantees.
